sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position Size = Account Equity / (Risk Amount / f). Example shows f=0.25 as optimal with TWR of 9.32. Apply fixed fractional position sizing consistently across all trades. Higher f increases drawdown risk.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing account equity by the fixed fractional amount, where f represents the optimal fraction of account equity to risk per trade.
